input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

A close-up reveals a silver camping stove with a black pot, packaged food, and additional orange bowls on the table. The news ticker scrolls various headlines at the bottom of the screen throughout the segment.

samples.output: <think>A close-up shot maintains focus on a white checklist document spread over orange bowls, featuring images and text for items like a backpack, food, and lantern. A hand holds the top edge of the paper steady. The yellow news ticker at the bottom scrolls left, displaying text about a Louisiana lawmaker wanting to cut state money.</think><answer></answer>

The presenter gestures with open palms, holds a green package, and manipulates orange collapsible bowls. A close-up reveals a silver camping stove with a black pot, packaged food, and additional orange bowls on the table. The news ticker scrolls various headlines at the bottom of the screen throughout the segment.

input.user_input: What rating does the graphic display for the hurricane gear test?

samples.output: <think>The static graphic displaying the hurricane kit details and price remains unchanged. The yellow news ticker at the bottom continues scrolling left, revealing the full city name "CHARLOTTE" followed by a period and the "FOX 2" logo appearing on the far right edge of the screen.</think><answer>The graphic displayed during the segment titled "JD's Hurricane Gear Test" explicitly shows a rating for the Sustain Supply Co 72 Hour Kit. This visual evidence indicates a score represented by three red hurricane symbols alongside two grey ones. The static screen maintains this specific rating configuration while also listing the product's price, clearly communicating the test results to the viewer through this symbolic scale.</answer>

metadata.gold_answer: The graphic displayed during the segment titled "JD's Hurricane Gear Test" explicitly shows a rating for the Sustain Supply Co 72 Hour Kit. This visual evidence indicates a score represented by three red hurricane symbols alongside two grey ones. The static screen maintains this specific rating configuration while also listing the product's price, clearly communicating the test results to the viewer through this symbolic scale.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[42-44] The presenter extends his right arm to point at packaged supplies on the table's right, looking at them while his left hand rests near orange bowls. The bottom news ticker scrolls left, displaying "MORE THAN 80 FACULTY MEMB". The yellow ticker scrolls, changing from 'MORE THAN 80 FACULTY MEMBERS AT THE UNIVERSITY OF' to '...NORTH TEXAS HAVE SIGNED A LETTER OPI'.

[4-8] The man raises both hands with open palms, then gestures toward the black backpack on the left, looking down at the gear. The ticker scrolls "FOX 26 houston.com" and "TEXAS UNITED WAY: 211". [8-12] A close-up shows the black backpack with a white circular "SUSTAIN GEAR" logo. A hand gestures near the bag on the right. The hurricane graphic remains behind, and the ticker reads "THE LAURA BUSH FOUNDATION FOR". [14-16] A presenter in a light blue shirt and patterned tie stands behind a table, reaching his right hand toward two orange bowls while the ticker displays "THE LAURA BUSH FOUNDATION FOR AMERICAN LIBRARIES" and background screens remain static. [16-18] The camera cuts to a close-up of the table showing four green packages labeled "6" SnapLight SAFETY LIGHTSTICK" and black plastic utensils, as a hand adjusts one package near orange bowl bases. [18-20] Returning to a wide shot, the man holds a

[12-14] A wide shot captures a presenter in a light blue shirt and patterned tie behind a table holding a black backpack and orange bowls, gesturing with raised hands. Background screens show "JD's Hurricane Gear Test" and a hurricane, while the ticker displays "THE LAURA BUSH FOUNDATION FOR AMERICAN LIB". [56-58] The camera cuts to a close-up of blue Mountain House food pouches, white emergency water packets, a grey pot, and a fire starter kit on the table. [58-62] Returning to the wide shot, the presenter stands with hands near the orange bowls as the ticker scrolls to read "UNIVERSITY OF NORTH TEXAS HAVE SIGNED A LETTER OPPOSING DONALD TRUMP JR. AS A SPEAKER".

[0-2] A graphic displays "JD's Hurricane Gear Test" with a red hurricane symbol and two red flags with black squares on the right, while a ticker reads "TEXAS UNITE!" over a stormy background. [62-66] The view shifts to a close-up of a silver camping stove holding a black pot, flanked by orange collapsible bowls on the left and packaged emergency food on the right. The presenter's torso in a light blue shirt is visible behind the table as the news ticker scrolls text regarding Donald Trump Jr., updating from "IN A DONOR-" to "DONOR-SPONSORED LI". [66-68] The camera cuts to a wide shot showing the presenter standing behind the table with his hands near the orange bowls, wearing a patterned tie, while the ticker fully reveals the phrase "DONALD TRUMP JR. AS A SPEAKER IN A DONOR-SPONSORED LECTURE SERIES".

[74-80] A presenter in a light blue shirt and patterned tie stands behind a table displaying hurricane gear, including a black backpack. He holds an orange collapsible bowl with both hands, lowering it towards the surface. Behind him, screens show "JD's Hurricane Gear Test" and a hurricane image. The yellow news ticker scrolls left, revealing "THE ASTROS BEAT THE RANGERS IN ARLINGTON YESTERDAY" next to "FOX 26 houston.com". [80-82] The presenter places the orange bowl on the table next to a second identical bowl and raises both hands with palms facing forward. The ticker updates to display the score "11-2" and the text "BOTH TE".

[82-84] The presenter gestures as the ticker displays "BOTH TEAMS PLAY AG" before cutting to "Fire Starter" packets, a red tool, and black utensil. [84-86] The shot holds on the packets while the ticker updates to "BOTH TEAMS PLAY AGAIN TODAY AT". [86-88] Returning to a wide shot, the presenter stands near two orange bowls as the ticker reveals "1:05 P.M. CEN". [88-90] A close-up shows a white "SUSTAIN FIRST AID KIT", black flashlight, and packaged orange knife being touched by a hand, while the ticker finishes "CENTRAL TIME."

[92-94] The view switches from a close-up of table items to a wide shot showing the presenter in a light blue shirt and tie. He stands behind the black-clothed table, gesturing with open hands near the orange bowls and black backpack. Background screens display a hurricane graphic and segment title. The yellow ticker continues scrolling sports information about a 1:05 P.M. start time.

The yellow news ticker continues scrolling left, now displaying "THE DALLAS COWBOYS" on the right side. The orange bowls, black backpack, and background screens featuring the hurricane graphic remain static in their positions.

You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
